title: DNA Extraction from Modern Dental Calculus
author: Franziska Aron
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7p8hmrw

steps.instruction: Weigh out 3 mg to 5 mg of the calculus and transfer it into a 2ml-Eppendorf Safe-Lock DNA LoBind-Tube (use special accuracy scales)
Crush the calculus with a sterile metal pestle within the tube.
Add 250 µL 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA and 12.5 µL Proteinase K ( 10 mg, /ml )

Let it rotate in the dark at Room temperature for 24:00:00, up to 48h (optional: over the weekend) until the calculus fully decalcifies and either disappears or becomes buoyant (floating and feathery in appearance)
Remove the tubes from the rotator and briefly centrifuge the extraction tubes This centrifugation step is just made to remove the liquid from the lid.
Transfer everything with a 1000 µL pipette tip (supernatant and pellet) to the Power Bead tubes
Invert to mix.
Add 60 µL C1 solution, invert several times to mix.
Secure tubes horizontally on a flat-bed vortex pad with tape, vortex at maximum speed for 00:10:00
Centrifuge at 9400 x g, °C for 00:00:30 at Room temperature Make sure the Power Bead tubes can rotate without rubbing, 10.000 x g is the maximum, otherwise the tubes may break
Transfer supernatant to the 2ml collection tube (provided in the kit) Expect 400 µL to 500 µL
Add 250 µL C2 Solution to the supernatant and vortex 00:00:05 at maximum speed
Incubate at 4 °C for 00:05:00 on the pre-cooled rack ( 4 °C )
Centrifuge 9400 x g, °C for 00:01:00 at Room temperature
Transfer 600 µL of the supernatant to a clean 2ml collection tube (provided in the kit), avoid transferring the pellet.
Add 200 µL C3 solution and vortex briefly
Incubate the tubes at 4 °C for 00:05:00 on the pre-cooled rack ( 4 °C )
Centrifuge the tubes at 9400 x g, °C for 00:01:00 at Room temperature
Transfer 750 µL of the supernatant to a clean 2ml collection tube (provided in the kit)
Gently shake C4 Solution to mix
Add 1.2 mL C4 Solution to supernatant and vortex for 00:00:05 The solution should not exceed the rim of the tube.
Bind the DNA on the membrane of spin filter unit:
Repeat until the entire solution has been passed through the spin filter unit (2-3 times) go to step #30.1
Load 500 µL C5 Solution into spin filter unit
Centrifuge 00:01:00 9400 x g, °C at Room temperature
Discard flow through
Dry spin 00:01:00 9400 x g, °C at Room temperature
Place the spin filter unit into a clean 2ml collection tube (provided in the kit)
Pipette 100 µL C6 Solution into center of the membrane in the spin filter unit and incubate for 00:01:00
Centrifuge 9400 x g, °C for 00:00:30 at Room temperature
Remove spin filter unit and quantify the DNA ( 3 µL ) with the Qubit ds BR Kit
Centrifuge for 00:01:00 9400 x g, °C at Room temperature
Remove supernatant Optional: Transfer the supernatant to a clean 1.5ml loBind Tube and stored it for future analysis at -20 °C
Note this step is optional, however it can be useful to remove exogenous fluids such as blood. Add 1 mL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA and let it rotate on a tube rotator for 00:15:00 at Room temperature

Clean the metal pestle after every sample by incubating for 00:02:00 in a household bleach dilution , then wiping clean with HPLC-water and drying with a clean paper towel go to step #8 Sterile plastic pestles can be used instead of metal pestles, but they must be changed between each sample and not re-used.
Save power bead tubes at -20 °C as backup. These can be thrown out once you know the extraction worked
Store remaining supernatant at -20 °C We save this as backup until confirmation that the extraction worked. If extraction was successful, this can be discarded.
Load approximately 675 µL into spin filter unit
Centrifuge 00:01:00 at 9400 x g, °C at Room temperature
Discard flow through
Store the eluate at -20 °C
Place a 1.5ml/2ml tube rack at 4 °C for the incubation steps on the final days
Prepare a 1:10 dilution of household bleach solution (~6% hypochlorite) in a 50ml-Falcon tube
Label for each sample one 2ml-Eppendorf Safe-Lock DNA LoBind-Tube and one 1.5ml-Eppendorf Safe-Lock DNA LoBind-Tube

Retrieve the spin filter columns (provided in the kit) from fridge to warm them up to Room temperature for use at step 26 "provided in the kit" refers to: DNeasy PowerSoil Kit (100) Cat No./ID: 12888-100 A spin filter column is a spin filter unit sitting in a collection column. The spin filter unit contains the membrane.
Label for each sample one Power Bead tube, one spin filter column and three 2ml collection tubes (all provided in the kit)
